name: Peucedramidae
description: Peucedramidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird found in montane forests from the southwestern United States to Nicaragua. The single species is a unique warbler-like bird with uncertain affinities to other passerine families.

children.name: Peucedramus
children.description: A monotypic genus of warbler-like passerine endemic to highland pine forests of North and Central America.

children.name: Peucedramus taeniatus
children.description: The Olive Warbler is a grey-and-yellow bird with a distinctive orange head in adult males. It inhabits high-elevation pine forests from Arizona to Nicaragua, foraging for insects among the needles.
